newsSince: No new company-specific catalyst since June 9. Industry backdrop remains strong: ABF substrate continues to sell out across Unimicron/Kinsus/Nan Ya PCB on AI-chip demand, and Nan Ya is ramping capacity. 1Q26 revenue NT$11.2B (+32% YoY), EPS NT$2.03; FY26 consensus ~NT$58.4B / EPS NT$14.91.

sources.label: Digitimes - ABF substrate sells out (Unimicron/Kinsus/Nan Ya)
Digitimes - Nan Ya PCB to ramp ABF capacity

verdicts.rankRationale: Master-list packaging-scarcity lens: ABF tightness and capacity ramp are real but already-known, with no new name-specific evidence since Jun 9 and price essentially flat on the period. At +284% YTD with sell-side targets already lofty, this is a crowding signal, not fresh hidden bottleneck alpha. Hold.
verdicts.bull: ABF substrate sold out; capacity ramp into structural AI-GPU/networking demand; 1Q26 rev +32%.
verdicts.neutral: Strong but consensus demand; rerating already extreme and monthly revenue trends in-line.
verdicts.bear: +284% YTD on a known sell-out narrative - residual alpha compressed, ABF execution/pricing risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: ABF capacity ramp; AI-GPU substrate demand
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding / extreme YTD rerating; substrate yield and pricing execution
